title: Improve type compatibility checking for dynamic arrays and queues
body:
SystemVerilog defines different levels of type compatibility.
 * Matching
 * Equivalent
 * Assignment compatible
 * Cast compatible

At the moment the `nettype_t` has only one type compatibility test. It is
used to check assignment compatibility when assigning to a dynamic array,
queue or class.

The current implementation rejects a few cases that should allowed and
allows a few cases that should be rejected.

Dynamic arrays and queues are assignment compatible if their element types
are compatible. And two packed types are equivalent if they are both
2-state or 4-state, both signed or unsigned and have the same packed with.

In the current implementation the sign is not considered and instead of
checking if the packed width is the same it checks that the dimensions are
identical.

Refactor the type checking to implement both equivalency and assignment
compatibility checks. This allows to correctly check the assignment
compatibility checks for dynamic arrays and queues.

Part of this series is also a small refactoring removing the `packed_width()`
method for the `netdarray_t` to make sure the vlog95 backend does not
trigger an assert when passing dynamic arrays or queues as arguments
to task. Dynamic arrays and queues are not support by the vlog95 backend,
so this will still fail. But it will fail gracefully with an error message rather than
a crash.
